opp.name: Veritiv_S2P_June26
opp.next_step: 6/12mc- Follow up with Mayra Contreras to confirm a 45-minute introductory meeting with Carlos Cárdenas, National Procurement Manager, and Edgar Barraza, National Sourcing Manager. Mayra agreed to speak with them and help coordinate time after reviewing the materials shared by email. Objective of next meeting: introduce Zycus at a more executive level, validate current procurement/sourcing pain points, and determine whether there is a business case for a more tailored demo around intake, sourcing, procurement orchestration, supplier visibility, and AI-driven automation.
opp.ais_why:
• Veritiv has the full agentic suite scoped (ANA, Agentic AI, Merlin, Merlin Intake) but no discovery description on the opportunity.
• The Agentic-platform-discussed flag is not set and the next step is simply arranging a meeting with buyer stakeholders.
• With scope but no engagement detail or AI sentiment, evidence supports only exploratory appetite.

items.quote: Carlos Cárdenas is named as National Procurement Manager and functional decision-maker, but budget authority for $200k software investment is unconfirmed. No CFO or finance sponsor mentioned. Mayra stated decisions are made independently in Mexico vs Atlanta, but approval chain is unknown.
items.source: Avoma call 2026-06-12 (inference from Mayra's description of Carlos as 'la cabeza' and decision-maker, but no mention of budget or finance approval)

items.quote: No formal decision process, committee, or timeline shared. Unknown: who else must be involved (IT, InfoSec, legal, finance), whether an RFP or multi-vendor eval is required, and what approval steps follow the demo.
items.source: Avoma call 2026-06-12 (no discussion of approval steps or eval process)

items.quote: Contracting mechanics, legal review, InfoSec requirements, and procurement channel unknown. No MSA, SOW, or InfoSec questionnaire mentioned.
items.source: Avoma call 2026-06-12 (no discussion of contracting or InfoSec)

items.quote: No business event, budget deadline, contract expiration, or urgent pain driver mentioned. Risk: deal drifts without urgency.

sources: Avoma call 2026-06-12, Mayra Contreras confirmation of manual follow-up workload
Avoma notes 2026-06-12: 'Centralizing requisitions into one intake (i2O) was identified as a key opportunity to improve visibility, automation and to generate savings within the first three months'
metrics.narrative: Pain is confirmed but not yet quantified. Veritiv Mexico procurement spends significant time on manual follow-ups and status inquiries. Mayra stated: 'Sí, sí, sí. De hecho, veo mi mi conversaciones ahí, como están esas preguntas' when asked if her team fields 'where is my PO / payment' questions constantly (source: Avoma call 2026-06-12). Zycus positioned centralizing requisitions into one intake (i2O) as a key opportunity to improve visibility, automation, and generate savings within first three months (source: Avoma notes 2026-06-12). No dollar savings target, cycle-time reduction, headcount efficiency, or error-rate baseline has been articulated by the buyer yet. The intro call established qualitative pain (manual, reactive work vs strategic) but the business case and ROI have not been quantified. Next move: use the 45-minute demo to extract quantified pain (current manual hours per week, number of requisitions, average cycle time, vendor count, spend under management) and co-develop a savings model.

sources: Avoma call 2026-06-12, Mayra Contreras expressing strong interest and taking ownership of next step
Avoma call 2026-06-12, Mayra committing to internal advocacy with Carlos and Edgar
Next Step (SF, 2026-06-12) confirming Mayra's commitment to coordinate demo after internal review
champion.narrative: Champion is developing but not yet secured. Mayra Contreras (procurement coordinator, Veritiv Mexico) is the current single point of contact. She showed strong positive sentiment and took ownership of the next step: 'para nosotros sí sería un muy buen, muy, muy buena herramienta que nos podría, este, evitar muchos, muchos dolores de cabeza' and 'Déjame, entonces, me llevo de tarea conseguirte, este, esos cuarenta y cinco minutos' (source: Avoma call 2026-06-12). Mayra is actively advocating internally—she committed to discuss the Zycus materials with Carlos Cárdenas (National Procurement Manager) and Edgar Barraza (National Sourcing Manager) and secure demo time. However, Mayra is not the decision-maker (she explicitly stated Carlos makes the decisions) and her role is coordinator/gatekeeper, not executive sponsor. She has no confirmed access to the Economic Buyer or budget authority. Current engagement: active as of 2026-06-12 (14 days ago); awaiting her follow-up on demo scheduling. What has developed: Mayra understands the pain Zycus solves, sees fit for Veritiv Mexico's needs, and is driving the internal conversation. What is missing: (1) Mayra has not yet delivered the demo confirmation (14 days elapsed, no update), (2) Carlos and Edgar have not engaged directly with Zycus, (3) no evidence Mayra is pushing back on competitors or sharing internal intel, (4) her influence over Carlos/Edgar's decision is unconfirmed. Next move: follow up with Mayra by 2026-06-28 (16 days post-call, within the 14-day horizon) to re-confirm her commitment and offer to facilitate scheduling directly with Carlos/Edgar if helpful. If no reply, escalate outreach to Carlos directly (his contact info may be available via LinkedIn or Veritiv public directory).

sources: Avoma call 2026-06-12, Mayra confirming no procurement platform in use, manual processes only
Avoma call 2026-06-12, Mayra confirming no AI procurement projects in flight in Mexico
competition.narrative: Primary competition is do-nothing (status quo). Veritiv Mexico currently has no procurement platform and relies on manual processes (WhatsApp, spreadsheets, email) per Mayra: 'No, todavía no. Cada quien está, ahora sí que cada quien nos nos entendemos con nuestros nuestros medios' and confirmed 'Exacto, algo así' when Mario summarized 'Whatsapp y spreadsheets y correos' (source: Avoma call 2026-06-12). No named software competitor (Coupa, SAP Ariba, GEP, Ivalua, Jaggaer) was mentioned. Mayra explicitly stated they do not use Coupa or similar when asked: 'No, todavía no' (source: Avoma call 2026-06-12). No active AI procurement projects exist in Mexico; Mayra stated: 'Específicamente, en compras, no tenemos ahorita proyectos en puerta… el tema el tema de la inteligencia artificial para para compras ahorita todavía no tienen un proyecto fuerte' and noted Atlanta is developing an AI initiative focused on sales, not procurement (source: Avoma call 2026-06-12). Do-nothing risk is real: if Carlos and Edgar do not see urgency or ROI, the deal stalls indefinitely. The wedge: Zycus must quantify the cost of the status quo (manual hours, missed savings, slow cycle times) and position the AI-native, Teams-embedded Merlin agent as a step-change vs remaining manual. No other vendor has been mentioned, but expect competition to emerge if Veritiv Mexico runs a formal RFP or eval process (Coupa, SAP Ariba, and GEP are the typical enterprise S2P shortlist in LATAM). Next move: during the demo, anchor the do-nothing cost and create urgency by tying Zycus to a business event (budget cycle, systems rationalization post-Landsberg integration, or a savings target Carlos owns).

sources: Avoma call 2026-06-12, Mayra Contreras confirming constant manual follow-up questions from internal stakeholders
Avoma call 2026-06-12, Mayra confirming no procurement platform in use, relying on WhatsApp/spreadsheets/email
Avoma notes 2026-06-12: sourcing and procurement functionally separate despite org unification
identify_pain.narrative: Specific operational pain confirmed: Veritiv Mexico procurement spends significant time on manual follow-ups and reactive work instead of strategic initiatives. When asked if her team constantly fields 'where is my PO / where is my payment' questions, Mayra confirmed: 'Sí, sí, sí. De hecho, veo mi mi conversaciones ahí, como están esas preguntas' (source: Avoma call 2026-06-12). Zycus noted this reactive workload prevents strategic focus: 'en vez de, a veces, de ser más estratégico, ustedes van, viene a ser más más, solamente atendiendo a la respuesta, más reaccionario' and Mayra agreed (source: Avoma call 2026-06-12). Second pain: lack of centralized intake and orchestration. Veritiv Mexico has no unified procurement tool; teams use 'Whatsapp y spreadsheets y correos' (Mayra confirmed when asked if they have a Coupa or similar platform; source: Avoma call 2026-06-12). Third pain: sourcing and procurement remain functionally separate despite organizational unification after the Landsberg acquisition (source: Avoma notes 2026-06-12). Pain owner: Carlos Cárdenas (National Procurement Manager) and Edgar Barraza (National Sourcing Manager) are the functional owners; Mayra is the coordinator experiencing the daily manual workload. Pain is confirmed and grounded in the buyer's words; quantification (hours/week, error rate, cycle time) is the next step.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics, legal review, InfoSec requirements, and procurement channel are unknown. No MSA, SOW, Order Form, redline process, or InfoSec questionnaire has been mentioned. No SI/partner involvement has been discussed. Veritiv Mexico operates a homegrown ERP (SAM) and Landsberg (acquired entity) uses SAP; Mayra stated integration experience has been friendly, but formal InfoSec / IT approval process for a new cloud platform (Zycus SaaS) is unconfirmed. No contract expiration or renewal forcing function exists (this is a new initiative, not a replacement). At this early stage (intro call completed 14 days ago, demo not yet scheduled), paper process visibility is not expected, but it must be uncovered during qualification. Next move: once the demo is confirmed and Carlos/Edgar engage, ask about Veritiv Mexico's software procurement process—does legal review contracts, does InfoSec run a security assessment, is there a preferred vendor onboarding process, and what is the typical contract cycle time.

sources: Avoma call 2026-06-12, Mayra Contreras describing Carlos Cárdenas as national procurement head and decision-maker
Avoma call 2026-06-12, Mayra stating Mexico makes independent decisions from Atlanta HQ
economic_buyer.narrative: Economic Buyer is unidentified. Carlos Cárdenas is named as National Procurement Manager and appears to be the functional decision-maker, but budget authority for a $200k S2P platform is unconfirmed. Mayra stated: 'Carlos Cárdenas… es el nuestro gerente, pues, nacional de de de compras… él es como el el el, la cabeza, ahora sí, nacional' and 'la idea que yo tengo es saber de qué se trata para podérsela exponer AAA él y, pues, ya él que tome las decisiones' (source: Avoma call 2026-06-12). This positions Carlos as the procurement head and gate-keeper, but does not confirm he controls the commercial or has final budget sign-off. For a $200k enterprise software deal, CFO or finance approval is likely required. Veritiv Mexico operates independently from Atlanta (decisions made separately per Mayra: 'Atlanta toma sus decisiones y México toma sus decisiones por tema de de conveniencia'), so the EB is likely Mexico-based. No finance sponsor, CFO, or C-level exec has been mentioned. The 45-minute demo is with Carlos and Edgar (sourcing head) but their budget authority must be validated. Next move: during the demo, ask Carlos directly about budget approval process, who signs off on software investments of this size, and whether finance/CFO involvement is required.

sources: Avoma call 2026-06-12, Mayra describing her gatekeeper role and Carlos/Edgar as decision-makers
Avoma call 2026-06-12, Mayra stating Mexico makes independent procurement decisions from Atlanta
Next Step (SF, 2026-06-12) confirming next milestone is demo with Carlos and Edgar after internal review
decision_process.narrative: Decision process is emerging but not yet formalized. The call sequence shows: (1) Mayra Contreras (procurement coordinator) took the intro call 2026-06-12 as a gatekeeper / information-gatherer, not a decision-maker. She explicitly stated her role is to understand Zycus and present it to Carlos Cárdenas (National Procurement Manager) and Edgar Barraza (National Sourcing Manager), who will decide whether to proceed: 'la idea que yo tengo es saber de qué se trata para podérsela exponer AAA él y, pues, ya él que tome las decisiones' (source: Avoma call 2026-06-12). (2) Next step is internal review by Carlos and Edgar of the presentation materials Zycus sent, followed by a 45-minute demo if they confirm interest. (3) Mayra stated decisions are made independently in Mexico vs Atlanta HQ: 'Atlanta toma sus decisiones y México toma sus decisiones por tema de de conveniencia, porque manejamos, este, sistemas diferentes' (source: Avoma call 2026-06-12), so the approval chain is Mexico-local. (4) No formal approval steps, committee structure, or timeline have been shared. Inferred process: intro → internal review → demo with functional heads → (unknown: eval, shortlist, EB/finance approval, legal/InfoSec, contracting). The SF field 'Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c' and 'Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c' were not queried (Q3 fields unavailable), so approval chain is unconfirmed from the relevant stakeholder move: map the full decision process during the demo—ask Carlos how Veritiv Mexico evaluates and selects software vendors, who else needs to be involved, and what the typical timeline is.

sources: Avoma call 2026-06-12, Mayra's positive reaction to Teams-native Merlin agent and no-new-system-to-learn positioning
Avoma call 2026-06-12, Mayra stating integration experience with homegrown ERP has been friendly
Avoma notes 2026-06-12: sourcing and procurement functionally separate; both heads (Carlos, Edgar) need to engage
decision_criteria.narrative: Early-stage; no formal RFP or evaluation criteria shared yet. Mayra's engagement signals that ease of use, AI-enablement, and integration-friendliness are initial filters. Mayra responded positively to the Merlin agent demo embedded in Microsoft Teams (Veritiv uses Teams per call): 'Me me acabas de dar como un panorama completo… Qué gusto lo de conocerlo' and saw value in avoiding a new system to learn (source: Avoma call 2026-06-12). Integration feasibility is important: Mayra proactively mentioned that their homegrown ERP (SAM) 'ha sido muy amigable… sí han hecho varias integraciones sin problemas' when asked about integration challenges (source: Avoma call 2026-06-12), signaling integration track record matters. Mayra also flagged that sourcing and procurement remain functionally separate despite org unification, and both Carlos (procurement) and Edgar (sourcing) need to see value (source: Avoma notes 2026-06-12). Inferred criteria: (1) ease of adoption (Teams-native), (2) integration with SAM and SAP (Landsberg side), (3) covers both procurement and sourcing workflows, (4) AI/automation to reduce manual follow-up load. Formal criteria (RFP weighting, technical requirements, vendor shortlist) will emerge in later stages. Next move: during the 45-minute demo, ask Carlos and Edgar to describe their top 3 challenges and what a successful solution must deliver in the first 6 months.

ai_fit_signal.summary: Veritiv Mexico is AI Curious. No active AI procurement projects exist today (Mayra: 'Específicamente, en compras, no tenemos ahorita proyectos en puerta… todavía no tienen un proyecto fuerte'), but Mayra showed strong interest in AI-enabled procurement after the Merlin agent demo (source: Avoma call 2026-06-12). Atlanta HQ is developing an AI initiative focused on sales (not procurement), so there is organizational awareness of AI but no procurement-specific roadmap. Veritiv Mexico is open to AI but not yet demanding it, which fits the 'AI Curious' tier. The Merlin positioning resonated because it solves real pain (manual work) with AI, not AI for AI's sake. No AIS score in Salesforce (field is null).

items.action: Follow up with Mayra Contreras to re-confirm her commitment to secure the 45-minute demo with Carlos and Edgar.

items.expected_effect: Re-engages Mayra and confirms whether Carlos/Edgar reviewed materials and are interested in the demo. If yes, locks demo date. If no reply or pushback, surfaces whether deal is stalling and triggers escalation to Carlos directly.

items.action: Identify Carlos Cárdenas and Edgar Barraza contact info and establish direct outreach channel as backup if Mayra does not respond.
items.trigger: Single-threaded through Mayra (coordinator, not decision-maker). Carlos and Edgar are the functional heads but have not engaged. 14 days since Mayra's commitment with no demo confirmation. Ghost risk is elevated if Mayra does not deliver.
items.expected_effect: Multi-threads the deal by establishing direct access to Carlos and Edgar. If Mayra does not deliver demo by 2026-06-30, Mario can reach out directly to Carlos/Edgar via LinkedIn or email, reducing dependency on a single contact and lowering ghost risk.

Creates urgency by anchoring the cost of do-nothing and gives Carlos/Edgar a concrete ROI to justify budget. Moves deal from discovery to validation.

Clarifies timeline expectations and surfaces any approval blockers (finance, legal, InfoSec) so Zycus can plan accordingly. Moves deal from discovery to qualification.

If custom work is required, scope it early and include it in the proposal. Removes a late-stage derail risk.

items: Positive: Strong interest from Mayra Contreras on intro call 2026-06-12. Mayra committed to internal advocacy and securing 45-minute demo with Carlos and Edgar. Quote: 'para nosotros sí sería un muy buen, muy, muy buena herramienta'.

Cooling signal: 14 days elapsed since Mayra's commitment (2026-06-12) with no demo confirmation. Mayra stated target was 'Monday after the meeting' (2026-06-15), which is now 11 days overdue. This is borderline—internal review + scheduling can take 2-3 weeks, but silence beyond 2 weeks often signals fading interest or internal deprioritization.
Fragile: Single-threaded through Mayra (coordinator, not decision-maker). Carlos and Edgar have not engaged. If Mayra does not deliver, deal stalls.
confidence_signals.summary: Deal is engaged but fragile. Positive signal: strong interest from Mayra (coordinator) on intro call 2026-06-12. Cooling signal: 14 days elapsed since Mayra's commitment to secure demo with Carlos and Edgar, with no update. This is borderline—within normal range for internal review but approaching the point where momentum risks fading. No red flags yet (no rejection, no competitor emergence, no long silence), but deal is single-threaded and dependent on Mayra delivering the next step.

north_star_verdict.math: Deal created 2026-06-12, same day as intro call. 14 days in Qualified stage. Close date 340 days out is appropriate for early discovery. No slip history. Pace is consistent with enterprise procurement cycle: intro call → demo confirmation → executive-level presentation → deeper discovery → shortlist/eval. Time-in-stage is normal for waiting on buyer to schedule next meeting.
evidence: Intro call completed 2026-06-12 with Mayra Contreras (Veritiv Mexico procurement coordinator). Mayra showed strong positive interest in AI-enabled procurement and Merlin agent demo. Quote: 'Me me acabas de dar como un panorama completo lo de de de Berlín. Qué gusto lo de conocerlo.' and 'para nosotros sí sería un muy buen, muy, muy buena herramienta que nos podría, este, evitar muchos, muchos dolores de cabeza.' (source: Avoma call 2026-06-12)

Quote: 'Déjame, entonces, me llevo de tarea conseguirte, este, esos cuarenta y cinco minutos, este, para para yo ya regresar con ustedes y decirles, sabe qué? Tal día, a tales horas, podemos hacer la reunión.' (source: Avoma call 2026-06-12)

Zycus sent presentation materials and product videos on 2026-06-12 per Mayra's request. Next Step (SF, updated 2026-06-12) confirms follow-up action: awaiting Mayra to coordinate time with Carlos and Edgar after their internal review.
14 days elapsed since intro call and last activity. This is within normal range for buyer internal review and scheduling coordination. Not yet a stall.
north_star_verdict.headline: Deal stalled 14 days waiting for buyer-scheduled demo. Mayra committed to coordinate Carlos and Edgar but has not returned with availability. Early enthusiasm exists but lack of follow-up signals low internal urgency.

flags: Do-nothing wedge requires quantified cost: Veritiv is manual-process today with no active procurement platform or AI initiative. Buyer acknowledged pain but inertia is strong without a quantified business case. Mitigation: on the demo, present a quantified savings model (time saved per requisition, reduction in maverick spend, faster cycle time) tied to Merlin intake centralization and ask Carlos and Edgar to validate the assumptions against their current-state data (requisition volume, cycle time, procurement FTE count). Make staying manual more expensive than adopting Zycus.
best_practice_check.summary: Early-stage discovery deal. Three win-strategy best practices to raise probability now: (1) Multi-thread beyond Mayra to Carlos and Edgar before the demo (reduces ghost risk and validates real interest). (2) Extract quantified pain and co-develop ROI model during the 45-minute demo (do-nothing is the main competitor; urgency comes from quantifying its cost). (3) Map the Economic Buyer and budget approval process during the demo (for $200k, CFO or finance sign-off is likely required in addition to Carlos).

competitive_position.summary: Do-nothing (status quo) is the primary competition. Veritiv Mexico has no procurement platform and uses manual processes (WhatsApp, spreadsheets, email). No named software competitor (Coupa, SAP Ariba, GEP) has been mentioned as of 2026-06-12. No active AI procurement projects in Mexico. Do-nothing is a high current threat because it requires zero effort and zero budget from the buyer; the deal depends entirely on Zycus creating urgency by quantifying the cost of remaining manual. Expect named competitors to emerge if Veritiv Mexico runs a formal RFP or eval (Coupa, SAP Ariba, GEP are the typical LATAM enterprise S2P shortlist), but as of today no vendor competition exists. Wedge: Zycus is AI-native and Teams-embedded (no new system to learn), and can generate savings within 3 months by centralizing intake. Activation: during the 45-minute demo, quantify the do-nothing cost (manual hours, missed savings, slow cycle times) and tie Zycus ROI to a business event Carlos/Edgar own (budget cycle, post-acquisition systems rationalization, savings target).

competitors.name: Do-nothing (manual processes)
competitors.quote: No tool, each of us uses our own means, Whatsapp, spreadsheets, emails. We are constantly asked where is my order, we spend time on that instead of strategic work.
competitors.how_we_win: Demonstrate Merlin intake orchestration (centralized requisitions, auto-routing, Microsoft Teams interface) on the 45-minute demo with Carlos and Edgar, contrast manual-process pain (status inquiries, fragmented tools, no visibility) with i2O outcome (3-month savings, unified sourcing+procurement workflow), and quantify the time/cost saved per requisition to make inertia more expensive than adoption.

competitors.name: Do-nothing (status quo: manual processes, no procurement platform)
competitors.quote: No, todavía no. Cada quien está, ahora sí que cada quien nos nos entendemos con nuestros nuestros medios. (Mayra confirming no platform in use) … Exacto, algo así. (agreeing with 'Whatsapp y spreadsheets y correos' summary)
competitors.how_we_win: Quantify the cost of do-nothing (manual hours per week, missed savings opportunities, slow cycle times, lack of spend visibility) and position Zycus as a fast-ROI, low-lift solution (AI-native, Teams-embedded, savings in first 3 months). Tie to a business forcing function (budget cycle, systems rationalization post-Landsberg acquisition, or a savings/efficiency target Carlos and Edgar own). Create urgency: the longer they wait, the more savings they leave on the table.

items.deliverable: Mayra Contreras to discuss Zycus materials internally with Carlos Cárdenas (National Procurement Manager) and Edgar Barraza (National Sourcing Manager), and confirm availability for 45-minute demo with Zycus
items.grounding_quote: Déjame, entonces, me llevo de tarea conseguirte, este, esos cuarenta y cinco minutos, este, para para yo ya regresar con ustedes y decirles, sabe qué? Tal día, a tales horas, podemos hacer la reunión. (Mayra Contreras committing to secure demo time)

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning was strong in the intro call. Zycus led with the AI-native, Teams-embedded Merlin agent as the centerpiece, and Mayra responded very positively. Veritiv Mexico has no active AI procurement projects, which positions Zycus as the first-mover and removes AI-skepticism risk. Mayra explicitly noted that Atlanta is developing an AI initiative focused on sales (not procurement), so there is no internal AI competition. The Merlin demo resonated because it solves Mayra's daily pain (manual follow-ups) with AI automation. No under-positioning detected; the AI story was central and well-received. Next step: during the 45-minute demo, deepen the AI value story by showing Carlos and Edgar how Merlin learns, automates sourcing, and delivers savings in the first 3 months (vs generic 'AI is cool' messaging).

items.quote: Me me acabas de dar como un panorama completo lo de de de Berlín. Qué gusto lo de conocerlo. (Mayra expressing excitement after Merlin agent demo)
items.criterion: Ease of adoption (no new system to learn, Teams-native)

items.quote: Sí, sí, sí. De hecho, veo mi mi conversaciones ahí, como están esas preguntas. (Mayra confirming her team constantly fields 'where is my PO' questions)
items.criterion: Reduce manual follow-up workload and reactive procurement work

items.quote: ha sido muy amigable. El sistema que que manejan es muy amigable, entonces, este, sí han hecho varias integraciones sin problemas. (Mayra describing prior integration experience)
items.criterion: Integration-friendliness with existing systems (homegrown ERP SAM, SAP)

items.quote: Fíjate que me acaba de, o sea, también me me me vino a la mente el equipo de de sourcing, porque estamos separados. El equipo de sourcing y el equipo de compras está relativamente separado. (Mayra noting both teams need to see value)
items.criterion: Cover both procurement and sourcing workflows (Carlos and Edgar both need value)

items.quote: Específicamente, en compras, no tenemos ahorita proyectos en puerta… todavía no tienen un proyecto fuerte. (Mayra confirming no AI procurement projects, signaling openness to Zycus as the first)
items.criterion: AI-enablement for procurement (though no active AI projects today)

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Veritiv Mexico's stated needs align well with Zycus's core value proposition. Pain confirmed: manual follow-up workload, no centralized intake, WhatsApp/spreadsheet-based processes. Zycus positioned AI-native, Teams-embedded Merlin agent to address these pains, and Mayra responded very positively. Integration-friendliness is important to Veritiv (homegrown ERP SAM, acquired Landsberg uses SAP); Mayra stated prior integrations have been smooth, which favors Zycus. No misalignment surfaced in the intro call. Key fit validation still needed: (1) Zycus-SAM integration feasibility (SAM is homegrown, not a standard ERP), (2) whether Zycus covers both procurement and sourcing workflows to Carlos and Edgar's satisfaction, (3) ROI case must be quantified and credible for a $200k investment.

hard.ais_why:
• Veritiv has the full agentic suite scoped (ANA, Agentic AI, Merlin, Merlin Intake) but no discovery description on the opportunity.
• The Agentic-platform-discussed flag is not set and the next step is simply arranging a meeting with buyer stakeholders.
• With scope but no engagement detail or AI sentiment, evidence supports only exploratory appetite.
hard.next_step: 6/12mc- Follow up with Mayra Contreras to confirm a 45-minute introductory meeting with Carlos Cárdenas, National Procurement Manager, and Edgar Barraza, National Sourcing Manager. Mayra agreed to speak with them and help coordinate time after reviewing the materials shared by email. Objective of next meeting: introduce Zycus at a more executive level, validate current procurement/sourcing pain points, and determine whether there is a business case for a more tailored demo around intake, sourcing, procurement orchestration, supplier visibility, and AI-driven automation.

If Mayra does not deliver the demo or leaves, deal stalls without multi-threading.

deltas.to: Quantify the pain and build urgency: Do-nothing (status quo) is the primary competitor. Veritiv Mexico has no procurement platform and no AI projects, so the de
deltas.subject: Quantify the pain and build urgency: Do-nothing (status quo) is the primary competitor. Veritiv Mexico has no procurement platform and no AI projects, so the deal depends on Zycus creating urgency by quantifying the cost of remaining manual. Best practice: during the 45-minute demo, extract baseline metrics (number of requisitions per month, average cycle time, manual hours per week on follow-ups, vendor count, spend under management) and co-develop a savings model (X hours saved, Y% cycle-time reduction, Z% spend visibility gained in first 3 months). Anchor the ROI to a business event Carlos/Edgar own (budget cycle, post-Landsberg systems rationalization, or savings target).

deltas.to: Map the Economic Buyer and approval process: Carlos Cárdenas is the functional procurement head but budget authority for $200k is unconfirmed. For enterprise so
deltas.subject: Map the Economic Buyer and approval process: Carlos Cárdenas is the functional procurement head but budget authority for $200k is unconfirmed. For enterprise software of this size, CFO or finance approval is typically required. Best practice: during the demo, ask Carlos directly: 'For a software investment of this size, what is the approval process? Who else needs to sign off—finance, CFO, IT, legal? What is the typical timeline from demo to contract signature?' Use his answer to map the full decision chain and identify the Economic Buyer early (vs discovering late-stage that another layer of approval exists).

value.deliverable: Send Zycus presentation materials and product videos to Mayra Contreras for internal review by Carlos Cárdenas and Edgar Barraza
value.grounding_quote: Next step agreed: Zycus will send presentation and videos; Veritiv (Mayra) will discuss internally with Carlos and Edgar and propose a 45-minute demo time. (Avoma notes 2026-06-12) … Lo que me encantaría hacer… yo me me encantaría poder mandarte esta presentación, y también te voy a mandar un par de vídeos. (Mario Castro committing on call)

packets.subject: Zycus:Send Zycus presentation materials and product videos to Mayra Contreras for internal review by Carlos Cárdenas and Edgar Barraza

value.name: Do-nothing (status quo: manual processes, no procurement platform)
value.quote: No, todavía no. Cada quien está, ahora sí que cada quien nos nos entendemos con nuestros nuestros medios. (Mayra confirming no platform in use) … Exacto, algo así. (agreeing with 'Whatsapp y spreadsheets y correos' summary)
value.source: Avoma call 2026-06-12, Mayra Contreras
value.how_we_win: Quantify the cost of do-nothing (manual hours per week, missed savings opportunities, slow cycle times, lack of spend visibility) and position Zycus as a fast-ROI, low-lift solution (AI-native, Teams-embedded, savings in first 3 months). Tie to a business forcing function (budget cycle, systems rationalization post-Landsberg acquisition, or a savings/efficiency target Carlos and Edgar own). Create urgency: the longer they wait, the more savings they leave on the table.

packets.subject: Do-nothing (status quo: manual processes, no procurement platform)

value.detail: No urgency driver or forcing function identified. Close date 2027-06-01 is 340 days out and realistic, but without a business event (budget deadline, contract expiration, savings target deadline), deal risks drifting indefinitely. Do-nothing competition is high when urgency is low.
value.source: Avoma call 2026-06-12 (no compelling event mentioned); close date 2027-06-01 is placeholder, not anchored to buyer timeline

packets.subject: No urgency driver or forcing function identified. value.detail: Economic Buyer unknown. Carlos Cárdenas is functional procurement head but budget authority for $200k is unconfirmed. For enterprise software, CFO or finance approval is typically required. Risk: late-stage discovery of additional approval layer delays or kills deal.
value.source: Avoma call 2026-06-12 (Carlos named as decision-maker but budget authority not validated)

packets.subject: Economic Buyer unknown. Carlos Cárdenas is functional procurement head but budget authority for $200k is unconfirmed. For enterprise software, CFO or finance approval is typically required. Risk: late-stage discovery of additional approval layer delays or kills deal.

value.detail: Sourcing and procurement remain functionally separate despite organizational unification post-Landsberg acquisition. Both Carlos (procurement) and Edgar (sourcing) must see value. If either does not buy in, deal scope narrows or stalls.
value.source: Avoma notes 2026-06-12: 'Sourcing and purchasing remain functionally separate despite organizational unification'

packets.subject: Sourcing and procurement remain functionally separate despite organizational unification post-Landsberg acquisition. Both Carlos (procurement) and Edgar (sourcing) must see value. If either does not buy in, deal scope narrows or stalls.

value.flag: Multi-thread: Single point of contact (Mayra Contreras, coordinator/gatekeeper) is a ghost risk. Carlos Cárdenas (National Procurement Manager) and Edgar Barraza (National Sourcing Manager) are the functional decision-makers but have not engaged directly with the relevant stakeholder practice: before or during the 45-minute demo, establish direct contact with Carlos and Edgar (LinkedIn connection, pre-demo email intro, or ask Mayra to include them on scheduling email thread). If Mayra does not deliver the demo or leaves, deal stalls without multi-threading.

packets.subject: Multi-thread: Single point of contact (Mayra Contreras, coordinator/gatekeeper) is a ghost risk. If Mayra does not deliver the demo or leaves, deal stalls without multi-threading.

value.flag: Quantify the pain and build urgency: Do-nothing (status quo) is the primary competitor. Veritiv Mexico has no procurement platform and no AI projects, so the deal depends on Zycus creating urgency by quantifying the cost of remaining manual. Best practice: during the 45-minute demo, extract baseline metrics (number of requisitions per month, average cycle time, manual hours per week on follow-ups, vendor count, spend under management) and co-develop a savings model (X hours saved, Y% cycle-time reduction, Z% spend visibility gained in first 3 months). Anchor the ROI to a business event Carlos/Edgar own (budget cycle, post-Landsberg systems rationalization, or savings target).

packets.subject: Quantify the pain and build urgency: Do-nothing (status quo) is the primary competitor. Best practice: during the 45-minute demo, extract baseline metrics (number of requisitions per month, average cycle time, manual hours per week on follow-ups, vendor count, spend under management) and co-develop a savings model (X hours saved, Y% cycle-time reduction, Z% spend visibility gained in first 3 months). Anchor the ROI to a business event Carlos/Edgar own (budget cycle, post-Landsberg systems rationalization, or savings target).

value.flag: Map the Economic Buyer and approval process: Carlos Cárdenas is the functional procurement head but budget authority for $200k is unconfirmed. For enterprise software of this size, CFO or finance approval is typically required. Best practice: during the demo, ask Carlos directly: 'For a software investment of this size, what is the approval process? Who else needs to sign off—finance, CFO, IT, legal? What is the typical timeline from demo to contract signature?' Use his answer to map the full decision chain and identify the Economic Buyer early (vs discovering late-stage that another layer of approval exists).

gaps: Q3 SOQL synthesis fields (MEDDPICC + competition Salesforce fields) were not queried, so Salesforce-side MEDDPICC and competitor data is unavailable. MEDDPICC and competition read is based solely on Avoma call 2026-06-12 content and Next Step field.
Only 0 buyer-side Opportunity Contact Roles on record (data-hygiene gap). Mayra Contreras, Carlos Cárdenas, and Edgar Barraza should be added as Contact Roles.
No OpportunityLineItem (product line items with pricing) data available, so product scope is based solely on Products__c field.
LastActivityDate is unknown in Salesforce (ground truth states 'unknown'), but Avoma call 2026-06-12 and Next Step update 2026-06-12 confirm last activity was 2026-06-12.
validation gate: a named person in a to-do/action item is in no Salesforce contact role, Avoma attendee list, or active-user roster and is anchored to no source (offending: 'Zycus. Best')
